Motion To Re-Open Plaintiffâ¿¿S Deposition For The Limited Purpose Of Deposing Plaintiff By Videotape Regarding The Declaration Submitted In Opposition To Rockwell Automation, Inc.Â¿¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicole E. Gage of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2105), appearing for defendant / moving party Rockwell Automation, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Frederick Spencer III

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF DEPOSING PLAINTIFF BY VIDEOTAPE REGARDING THE DECLARATION SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION TO ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion to re-open plaintiff's deposition for the limited purpose of deposing plaintiff regarding the declaration submitted in opposition to its motion for summary judgment is denied. Although the relevant law was not cited or discussed by defendant, plaintiff's deposition may only be re-opened for good cause shown. (C.C.P. 2025.610.) Defendant failed to demonstrate such good cause as it failed to properly question plaintiff during his deposition. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated good cause, the motion would still be denied because C.C.P. 2024.050, the statute upon which defendant moves and which is necessary here because discovery is closed, states that a motion brought under this section "shall be accompanied by" a meet and confer declaration. Defendant did not submit such declaration nor did it show that it attempted to resolve this issue informally with plaintiff's counsel prior to filing this motion. Defendant violated C.C.P. 2024.050. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
